creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still reproduces. Comparing a large int to its float representation gives a wrong result because the int is converted to float, losing precision.

**Repro:**
x = 9223372036854775807
print(x == 9223372036854775807.0)  // true (WRONG)
// the float is actually 9223372036854776000 (rounded up)

Edge case, only relevant at int64 bounds.

**Design question:** stricter cross-type equality, arbitrary precision, or accept/document this IEEE-754 behavior.
title: Large int vs float comparison gives wrong result due to precision loss
